In this lesson, I want to show you how to get started tracking using HyperTracker. Now once you've set up a HyperTracker account and you've logged in, the very first thing that you want to do is click on Campaigns and then you're going to click Create to create a new campaign. Now when you create a new campaign, the first thing that you do is start with the Campaign ID. Now remember, what we're doing here is we're going to be tracking each individual advertisement, each individual paid traffic source that you use separately. So for example, if you are doing Facebook advertising, maybe you'll have three different ads that you're running or maybe you're targeting three different segments of your market. Well, if that's the case, you need to have a separate tracking campaign for each of those three ads, for each of those three different audiences. Because what that does for you is it will show you which of the three ads or which of the three audiences are profitable for you, which ones are working for you. And that is critical. You don't want to use the same tracking link for every ad, every campaign that you run in Facebook or for every ad or every campaign that you run in Google AdWords or for every banner. Instead, you want to make sure that you're tracking each individual channel and each individual source separately. So we first want to start, like I said, with a Campaign ID. Now the Campaign ID is really just an identifier. It's the thing that is going to be included in the tracking link. Now you don't overthink this. You don't really need to create something that is crazy in terms of an ID. What we typically do is we'll go with something like let's say FBAD and then we might say whatever, you know, version one, let's say. Let's just do it like this. FBAD version one. Then what we do is we enter a name. And so over here, I'm going to go Facebook ad version, version one, and then I'll put in the date. And so let's put in, this is just to help you now so that you can see on your campaign list, the names of the campaign. So it gives you insight into what it is that this campaign is all about. Then I might say over here in the description, this is the first version of XYZ ad. Now over here under group name, this is if you want to organize your campaigns. So this is just for you and your organization. So if maybe you have, you might have banners that you want to go with. That's one group you might have. You might want to categorize by channel Facebook. So all of your Facebook campaigns go under that group. And again, that's just for your organization because later on, you'll be able to use that information. You'll be able to pull up all the campaigns in a particular group and so on. Now the next section is the campaign password and you don't need this at all. And so just get rid of that. Now the next section is where you enter the cost. Now there's two different ways to enter the cost data when tracking and campaign. The first way is if you're doing PPC or pay per click. So for example, if you're running a Facebook ad and you're paying per click and you are paying 50 cents per click, let's say, because that's what you enter into the data to your budget. Well, you simply come in here and you type 50 cents and then you check this little box over here so that it is a PPC campaign. Now when you do this, what hyper tracker will do is every click that you get on your tracking link, the link that you create for this campaign, it'll add 50 cents in the cost column for you. So every time the tracking link gets clicked, it'll add whatever you put in here. The second way to enter your campaign cost is if it's a fixed cost. So for example, if you pay, let's say, $100 for an email solo ad or you pay $300 for a banner placement for the month, let's just say, when you're doing it fixed cost and it's not based on per impression or per click or anything like that, then you simply enter the total cost of that ad right in here and you don't check this box. Now what I will tell you is that in some instances, you might not know what your per click cost will be. For example, in Facebook, you might see that you're entering 60 cents or 50 cents as a max cost per click, but maybe you only pay, you only end up paying 30 cents a click. Well, there's a couple of things that you can do. Number one, you can always go back into your campaign later, click on edit, edit that campaign, and then go in here and change it from 50 cents to 30 cents. The other thing that you can do is you can leave this campaign cost as zero to start with and then just regularly go back into your Facebook advertising platform and see how much you've spent. Then take the total amount that you've spent to date, come back into HyperTracker, click on edit, and then enter that new number. It might go from $50 to $60 to $80 to $100. You can simply come back in here and just change this campaign cost and it will update the metrics for you and show you the new return on investment, the new profit, and so on. The next section is count clicks. I recommend that you just leave this once per session. What this means is that this will count the total number of clicks that this campaign gets. If we left this on always, that means if somebody clicked on that tracking link four times, it will count those as four unique clicks or four different clicks in your campaign. We don't want that because it will skew your numbers. It will skew your opt-in rate. It will skew the metrics for this campaign. We typically leave this as once per session. In some cases, what we'll do is leave this as once per year. That means once per year means that all these timeframes are based on, once per session means if somebody comes to your website and they click that tracking link again two minutes earlier, it's not going to count as an additional click. It's only going to count as one click. Once per year means that it doesn't matter how many times in a year they're going to click on that link. It's still going to only count as one unique click. The last section over here is enter your target URL. For you, this is the URL of your traffic activation page. This is your website.com forward slash traffic captivation page. What this does over here is this will, HyperTracker will now, when we create this campaign, HyperTracker will give you a tracking link and it will say, hypertracker.com forward slash blah, blah, blah, and it will have your campaign ID in there. If you click on that link, it will redirect them to this particular URL. This is where you want to send visitors through your tracking. A cool feature of HyperTracker that is not included with EdTracks Gold, which is another tracking platform that is very, very similar to HyperTracker. Another feature is that you can actually split test pages. There is split testing, which you're going to learn about inside the Six Figure Funnel Formula Training Program. Another option is that you can add a second link in here. For example, let's say that you've got a second version of your traffic activation page and you want to see which version generates more leads, which version generates more opt-ins, meaning which version has a higher percentage of visitors entering their email address to go into your funnel. You could simply leave this right in here and then in your tracking metrics, you'll be able to see which one of these two pages produced a higher result. HyperTracker will automatically split the traffic between these two pages automatically for you. Now, to get started, I just recommend that you leave your one URL in there for your traffic activation page. The next step is that you simply click Create Campaign. What you'll see is that, like I said, HyperTracker gives you a tracking link. This is the link that you're going to use for this particular ad. We said Facebook Ad Version 1. Now when we go and we create the Facebook Ad, we create the Facebook Advertising Campaign inside of the Facebook Advertising Platform. We set up Version 1 of the ad. When we enter the destination URL, the page that we want to send people to when they click on our Facebook Ad, we're going to use this link right here. By using this link, it's going to track everything that's going on. There are three additional steps that you need to take to complete your tracking. The first is we need to generate what's called lead tracking code. That's going to track the number of people that actually opt in, that actually enter their email address on your traffic activation page. In order to do that, we go up to integration. We click that button. What you'll see is a couple of options that we have. It says, what do you want to track? There are two things that we can track. We can track sales, which we're going to do next, or we can track what HyperTracker calls actions. For you are your opt-ins. When you see actions, just know that that is the number of people that have entered their email address. That's what we're going to be tracking. That's the action that we're going to look at. We click this radio button right here to track actions. Now it says, enter the product or action name. Over here, you would enter the name. In this case, we just say opt-in. Then over here, you might put in a description, opt-in for traffic, activation page, version one, let's just say. Over here, there is no value. This is for, you could see it says, this is product value. There is no action value. We just have to put in 0.00, leave this duplicate protection set to default, and then click show HTML. You have two different options for the tracking code that you can use. Both do the same thing. I recommend that you use this bottom one over here, this image tracking. All we do here is we take this tracking code right here, we copy it, and then we go over to your customer captivation page, the page that prospects come to after they fill out their email on your traffic captivation page. We take this, and we're going to paste this onto the customer captivation offer page. What happens is, somebody will click on the ad, your Facebook ad, they'll go through the hyper tracker link that we just got. Now when they land on your customer captivation page, this code will be fired. It'll show a pixel, and then it'll track back to the specific campaign that there was an action or an opt-in. We show you where you paste this on your page. This is very easy. Once on your customer captivation page, all you need to do is click text or some versions of WordPress say HTML, and then just paste it right in there. You just want to make sure that you're pasting this inside the HTML, the code version of the page. Some WordPress applications, some WordPress plugins, like for example, OptimizePress, will have a section that says code or scripts. You can just go ahead and paste that right in there. If you're given two different options for where to paste the code, whether it's in the header or the footer section, you want to go ahead and go with the header section. Now let me show you how to generate the sales code. Works the same exact way. We go back to the integration section. We leave this section on sales, and now you enter the name of the product. This would be the name of your customer captivation product. Over here you can enter a description. Over here is where you enter the value of the product. What is the price that the product sells for? In your case, if the product sells for $26, you enter this without the dollar symbol, leave this on default, and then click Show HTML. Once again, you're going to take this, and now you're going to paste this code on the page that your customers will come to after they take advantage of your customer captivation offer. In this case, there's two options. After your customer captivation page, prospects will have the ability to click and add to cart button and process their order. Maybe at the very beginning when you're creating your six-figure funnel, you're not using right out of the gate the transaction maximization sequence. Maybe you just send people to a thank you page that says, thank you for your purchase. If that's the case, you want to take this code and you want to paste it right onto that page, the HTML section like I just showed you, of the thank you page. What that'll do is that will track the number of sales and that will track the revenue generated. This is what will allow you to see how many sales you're getting from your visitors, from your opt-ins, as well as the total dollar value of the sales generated for this campaign. Like you're going to learn later on, this is how you're able to determine the ROI of a campaign. By inputting the cost, by tracking the total sales, you're able to see which traffic sources are viable and which ones are not. Now, let me tell you a little hack that will allow you to track the add-on offers in your funnel as you've built those out. Let's say that you've got your transaction maximization sequence in place. When you have the transaction maximization sequence in place, after the order form, individuals will come to your first add-on offer, your reverse add-on offer. In that case, you want to take this code from the customer captivation offer and you want to paste it into your reverse add-on offer page. Because when somebody hits your reverse add-on, that means that they've completed the customer captivation offer and so we want to track the customer captivation offer sales. We put this code on the reverse add-on page so it tells us, hey, someone's here. That means they bought the prior offer, the customer captivation offer. Now, how do you track the reverse add-on offer? Well, what you do is you create, you go back to integration again, you go back to the integration code and this time you'll enter the name of your reverse add-on. Then what you'll do is you'll enter the price point of your reverse add-on. Same thing, leave this as default and then click show HTML. Now, there's one tiny change that you need to make to this code and that is right over here, you'll see that it says sales equal one. Well, if you leave this as is, what will happen is when somebody takes your customer captivation offer, it'll show one additional sale under the campaign and then if they take the reverse add-on offer, it will show another sale. It'll actually look like two people bought instead of just one. What you want to do is just change this one right there to zero. What this will do is it won't count this as an additional sale, but it will still track the $19 of revenue towards your overall sales volume and towards your average transaction size. You want to make sure that you do that, you change this one to a zero for each of your add-on offers. For this particular one, the reverse add-on, of course, we're going to take this code and we're going to paste it on the page that your customers come to after this, meaning if they take advantage of this offer, what is the next page that they go to? If you happen to have a soft offer add-on, well, then you're going to paste this code onto that page because we know that if somebody reaches the soft offer add-on, that they've bought the reverse add-on and so we want to track the sales revenue. That's how you use HyperTracker to track all of your campaigns.
